title: perityle cernua
intro: Perityle cernua, also known as the desert marigold, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The medicinal properties of Perityle cernua lie in its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory and antiseptic agent, making it useful for wound healing and soothing irritated skin. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce fever, ease respiratory issues, and provide antioxidant protection. Perityle cernua can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for its medicinal uses, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
